text.title: vui-testing-best-practices Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for vui-testing-best-practices as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Reporting "100% intent accuracy on test set" as launch-ready without real-world participant testing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Synthetic test utterances correlate poorly with real-world speech distributions. Internal-team test sets over-represent the model's own training distribution. Require at least one device-loop test with external participants before launch.

failure-mode.description: Using auto-graded scoring as the sole success criterion without a human review pass.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Auto-graded scoring underweights tone, empathy, and dialog naturalness. Use a separate sentiment pass before declaring "pass", and hand 20 random failures to a designer for root-cause categorization.

failure-mode.description: PII redaction happening after transcripts are passed to the agent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Voice IP/PII redaction must happen BEFORE transcripts reach any LLM. Bake redaction into the runner as a mandatory pre-step, not a post-step. Audio files often contain names, phone numbers, and account details.

failure-mode.description: Simulator success declared as device success without device-loop validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Endpointing differences between simulator and physical device mean simulator success does not guarantee device success. Require at least one full device-loop test per major release.
